title: [Bug] Unable to select text inside database text cell in Firefox
author: LucasXu0
body:
### Bug Description

In Firefox, text inside a database text cell cannot be selected. Clicking and dragging within the cell does not highlight/select the text, so it's not possible to copy or edit a portion of it. This works as expected in other browsers (e.g. Chrome).

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open AppFlowy Web in Firefox.
2. Open a Grid/Database view.
3. Open a text cell that contains some text.
4. Try to select the text by clicking and dragging the cursor over it.

### Expected Behavior

The text inside the database text cell should be selectable, allowing the user to highlight, copy, and edit it, just like in Chrome.

### Browser and Version

Firefox

### AppFlowy Version(s)

Latest

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Logs and Console Output

_No response_

### Additional Context

https://discord.com/channels/903549834160635914/1343840905614069840/1519900163668316250

title: [Bug] Editing code blocks is very buggy
author: matous-volf
body:
### Bug Description

When I try to edit a code block with a specific code, it behaves extremely strangely and I absolutely cannot do the changes I wish.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a new page.
2. Create a code block.
3. Paste
   ```rs
               for page_id in new_book.page_ids.iter() {
                 /*
                 a part of the key. */
   ```
   into the block.
4. Try to insert a space after the dot in the comment.
5. Observe
   - the cursor jumping to `new_book`,
   - the removal of the `_` from `page_id`,
   - the italics (should not appear in a code block).

### Expected Behavior

The editing should behave normally.

### Browser and Version

Firefox 151.0.4

### AppFlowy Version(s)

cloud 0.15.22, web 0.14.9

### Screenshots

(I'm pressing space when focused in the code block and the cursor is just after `.`.)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6b205061-2229-4da4-8b71-234a751bf55b

### Logs and Console Output

On Ctrl+Z:
```
Invalid delete positions (start: 20, end: 20) for text length 20, skipping text deletion
```

### Additional Context

It seems it is related to the underscores and applying them as italics formatters.

title: [Bug] Refactor this function to not always return the same value.
author: telebovich
body:
### Bug Description

The intention of this function is not clear. Take a look.
``` JavaScript
  const shouldShowForCurrentView = useCallback(() => {
    if (!currentView) return true;
    
    // Show for all views
    return true;
  }, [currentView]);
```

Did you mean false in the `if` statement?

### Steps to Reproduce

Take a look at the function and tell if there is any logic in it. What was the original intent?

### Expected Behavior

Should return different values or this is not needed at all.

### Browser and Version

Edge latest

0.15

title: [Bug] Calendar view does not display selected properties in card preview (only visible after clicking item)
author: MaguraAndrei
body:
### Bug Description

Issue Summary:
In Calendar view, users can select properties to display on calendar items, but these properties are not shown directly on the calendar cards. Instead, only the title is visible, and additional information is only accessible after clicking the item. This makes the calendar view non-configurable in practice and limits its usefulness as a visual overview

### Steps to Reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:
Create a Calendar view 
Add items with multiple properties (e.g. title, tags, number, status, etc.).
Open Calendar view settings → Properties.
Select or unselect different fields (e.g. hide title, keep only tags or numbers).
Return to calendar view.
Click on an item to view details. 
Actual Result:
Calendar cards always show only the title
Even if other properties are selected, they are not displayed in the calendar view
Additional selected properties are only visible after clicking into the item
Hiding the title does not allow other fields to replace it visually
Calendar view behaves like a “title-only preview + click for details” system

### Expected Behavior

Expected Result:
Selected properties should be visible directly in the calendar card (not only after clicking)
Users should be able to define what is shown in the calendar preview (e.g. tags, numbers, status, etc.)
If title is hidden, other selected properties should replace it
Calendar cards should act as a summary view, not just a clickable title placeholder
Impact:
Reduces the Calendar view to a minimal navigation layer instead of an information view
Forces unnecessary clicks to access basic information
Makes property customization misleading since changes are not reflected visually
Weakens usability compared to tools like Notion, where calendar cards show meaningful summaries

### Browser and Version

Chrome Final Version/other browsers as well 

### AppFlowy Version(s)

Android App Final version

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Logs and Console Output

_No response_

### Additional Context

Suggested Improvement:
Implement configurable calendar card previews (field-based display)
Allow multiple properties to be shown directly on calendar items
Ensure hidden title can be replaced by selected fields
Make calendar cards function as informational summaries, not just links to details

title: [Bug] Password login succeeds locally, but AppFlowy-Web loses token on first /app load and redirects to /login
author: marcuspahlman
body:
### Bug Description

I am running AppFlowy Cloud locally with Docker and AppFlowy Web locally in dev mode.

The password login flow appears to succeed, but after redirecting to `/app`, the app immediately redirects back to `/login`. From debugging, the problem seems to be that the token is already `null` on the first auth check after navigation.

### Setup

AppFlowy Cloud:

* local Docker deployment
* `FQDN=localhost`
* `SCHEME=http`
* public base URL is `http://localhost`

AppFlowy Web:

* running locally in dev mode on `http://localhost:3000`
* configured to use local Cloud endpoints

### Relevant web env

```env
APPFLOWY_BASE_URL=http://localhost
APPFLOWY_GOTRUE_BASE_URL=http://localhost/gotrue
APPFLOWY_WS_BASE_URL=ws://localhost/ws/v2
```

### What works

Direct auth request works from terminal:

```bash
curl 'http://localhost/gotrue/token?grant_type=password' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email":"admin@example.com","password":"password"}'
```

This returns a valid `access_token`, `refresh_token`, and user object.

In the browser login flow, I also see logs showing:

* `signInWithPassword: starting`
* `GoTrue returned tokens, verifying with AppFlowy Cloud`
* `token verified and saved`
* `success, calling afterAuth`
* `afterAuth: redirecting to saved destination {pathname: '/app'}` 

### What fails

After redirecting to `/app`, the first auth check logs:

```text
[debug] auth check initial token raw null
[AppAuthLayer] redirecting to /login because auth check failed (double-checked)
```

and then `invalidToken()` is called as part of logout cleanup. 

So the token save path appears to run successfully, but by the time `/app` mounts, `localStorage.getItem('token')` is already `null`. The redirect back to `/login` seems to be a consequence of that, not the original cause. 

### Relevant code paths

From local debugging:

* password login goes through `src/components/login/EnterPassword.tsx` into `AuthService.signInWithPassword(...)` 
* the main logic is in `src/application/services/js-services/http/gotrue.ts` 
* token save uses `saveGoTrueAuth(JSON.stringify(data))` and writes to localStorage key `token` 
* `AppAuthLayer` checks auth using `isTokenValid()`, which just checks whether localStorage key `token` exists 



### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start AppFlowy Cloud locally with Docker.
2. Start AppFlowy Web locally in dev mode.
3. Open the web app.
4. Log in with password.
5. Observe successful login logs and redirect to `/app`.
6. Observe immediate redirect back to `/login`.
7. Observe that the first auth check on `/app` sees token `null`.

### Expected Behavior

After successful password login and token verification, `/app` should load as authenticated.

### Actual

Login succeeds, but `/app` immediately redirects back to `/login` because the initial auth check sees no token.

If helpful, I can also share the exact debug logs and local config I used.

### Browser and Version

Chrome 147.0.7727.102 (Official Build)

### AppFlowy Version(s)

0.12.17, 0.12.16

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Logs and Console Output

[Auth] signInWithPassword: starting {email: 'admin@example.com'}
[Auth] signInWithPassword: GoTrue returned tokens, verifying with AppFlowy Cloud
[Auth] signInWithPassword: token verified and saved
[Auth] signInWithPassword: success, calling afterAuth
[Auth] afterAuth: redirecting to saved destination {pathname: '/app'}
Navigated to http://localhost:3000/app
Error: User unauthorized
    at getCurrentUser (src/application/services/js-services/http/user-api.ts:26:29)
    at async revalidateUser (...)
    at async getCurrentUserCached (...)
    at async AppConfig.tsx:76:9
[AppAuthLayer] redirecting to /login because auth check failed (double-checked) {path: '/app', isAuthenticated: false, hasToken: false, hasContext: true}

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Bug] Page scroll jumps to top when pressing Enter inside a lower code block
author: PrimeHunter
body:
### Bug Description

When editing a code block that is located further down on a page, pressing the Enter key to create a new line causes the page to automatically scroll back to the top. This disrupts the editing experience, especially when working with longer documents.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a page with enough content to enable scrolling.
2. Scroll down to a code block positioned lower on the page.
3. Click inside the code block.
4. Press Enter to insert a new line

### Expected Behavior

The cursor should move to the next line within the code block, and the page should remain at the current scroll position.

### Browser and Version

Edge 146.0.3856.84

### AppFlowy Version(s)

Current AppFlowy Web version

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Logs and Console Output

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [FR] Cloning
author: craigkeefner
body: ### Feature Description I run PopOS Linux and nice app. I run kiosk association and write a lot. No team members. I'm trying AF and so far very impressed. Basically use for notes and daily task lists (still to do and completed). I wish I could set Sort By for Daily Tasks. I basically "clone" duplicate yesterdays entry and then have to rename it to todays date and then have to move it up the list to top. I will check on archiving or filing in folder past daily entries. I need to look at ToDo Doing and Done tags I guess. Very impressed for sure. I was going to buy a license but doesn't look like much value monthly for someone like me. Maybe I will donate some money in support ### Use Cases Keeping daily logs ### User Impact Solo operators and users ### Potential Implementation Ideas _No response_ ### Additional Context _No response_

title: [Bug] Share Overlay doesn't open because of the New Page overlay
author: MayurSMahajan
body:
### Bug Description

When creating a new page, the new page opens up as an overlay or modal. And on this page in the title, there is a Share button. Upon clicking the Share button another overlay just flashes, it opens and closes too quickly for the user to interact with it.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a new page inside your workspace
2. Observe how a modal opens up where you can enter contents of the page
3. Click the Share button in the top right corner
4. Observe the bug

### Expected Behavior

The Share modal should stay open if opened until the user closes it. If this is not possible, 
then convert the page modal to a page and then open the share modal.

### Browser and Version

Firefox 147

### AppFlowy Version(s)

Latest Web App

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4a96f41b-e0a4-4ff9-8959-dabd14379e0f
